_meta.note: Frozen offline labels for schema-RAG. Regenerate deliberately; never per-query. See index/labels.py.

campaigns.summary: Tracks marketing campaigns, their channels, spend, and start dates to analyze marketing performance.
campaigns.distinct_from: promotions – promotions track discount codes and active status, whereas campaigns track spend and channel information

categories.summary: Stores the hierarchy of product categories used for cataloging items.
categories.distinct_from: product_catalog, vendors – categories contains parent hierarchy while those tables only reference category names

customers.summary: Stores customer demographic and contact information for analysis of customer base and signup trends.

employees.summary: Stores employee records, including personal details, department assignment, hire date, and salary.
employees.distinct_from: customers – employees include department, hire_date, salary whereas customers include email, region, signup_date

inventory.summary: Tracks current stock levels of each product at each warehouse
inventory.grain: one row per product per warehouse
inventory.distinct_from: produced_items – records production events, not current stock levels

invoices.summary: Tracks invoices issued to customers, including amount and status, for billing and revenue analysis.
invoices.distinct_from: orders – invoices are billing documents while orders represent purchase transactions

manufacturing.summary: Tracks daily production output per plant, enabling analysis of manufacturing throughput.
manufacturing.grain: one row per plant per run date (production run).
manufacturing.distinct_from: produced_items – produced_items records individual items produced, whereas manufacturing aggregates output by plant and date.

order_items.summary: Tracks individual products and quantities within each customer order, enabling analysis of order composition and item-level revenue.
order_items.grain: one row per order line (each product in an order).
order_items.distinct_from: orders – orders aggregates totals, while order_items provides line‑item detail.

orders.summary: Tracks individual customer orders and their total amounts.
orders.distinct_from: invoices – orders record the purchase itself, while invoices record the billing statement for a customer

payments.summary: Tracks individual payment transactions for orders, enabling analysis of payment methods, amounts, and dates.
payments.distinct_from: orders (records order details, not payment), refunds (records refund amounts, not original payment)

produced_items.summary: Tracks the quantity of each product produced on each production date, enabling analysis of manufacturing output and inventory replenishment.
produced_items.grain: one row per item per production date

product_catalog.summary: Provides the list of products, their categories, and list prices for pricing and cataloging purposes.
product_catalog.distinct_from: categories (contains category hierarchy, not product details); inventory (contains stock levels, not list price)

promotions.summary: Stores promotional codes, their discount percentages, and active status for marketing campaigns.
promotions.distinct_from: campaigns – campaigns track spend and channel, while promotions track discount codes and percentages

purchase_orders.summary: Track procurement orders placed to suppliers, including order date and amount.
purchase_orders.distinct_from: orders – purchase_orders record supplier orders, not customer orders; invoices – purchase_orders are procurement orders, invoices are customer billing

refunds.summary: Track monetary refunds issued for each order
refunds.distinct_from: returns – refunds are linked to orders, returns are product-level

regions.summary: Stores geographic region identifiers, names, and associated countries for use in regional segmentation and reporting.
regions.distinct_from: customers (region column is a customer’s region, not a master list of regions)

returns.summary: Tracks individual product return transactions to analyze return volume and revenue impact.
returns.distinct_from: refunds – refunds are linked to orders, whereas returns record product-level return details

revenue_targets.summary: Defines the revenue target for each quarter to guide financial planning.
revenue_targets.distinct_from: sales_forecast – uses month and forecast_revenue instead of quarter and target

reviews.summary: Stores individual product reviews, including rating and date, to analyze customer feedback.

sales.summary: Track daily product sales revenue and cost to analyze profitability.
sales.grain: one row per sale transaction (product sold on a specific date).
sales.distinct_from: order_items – sales records actual sale transactions with revenue and cost, whereas order_items records order line items.

sales_forecast.summary: Provides forecasted revenue amounts for each month.

shipments.summary: Tracks shipping events for orders, including date, carrier, and status.
shipments.distinct_from: orders (shipments record shipping details, orders record order placement)

subscriptions.summary: Tracks which plan each customer is subscribed to and when the subscription started.
subscriptions.grain: one row per subscription record (customer‑plan start).
subscriptions.distinct_from: customers – subscriptions record plan and start date, whereas customers record personal details and signup date.

suppliers.summary: Stores information about suppliers, including their lead times and country of origin.
suppliers.distinct_from: vendors – vendors have a category field but no lead_time_days

tickets.summary: Track customer support tickets to analyze volume, priority, and status over time.
tickets.distinct_from: customers (tickets contain status and priority, customers contain personal info)

vendors.summary: Provides a master list of vendors and their categories for vendor management and reporting.
vendors.distinct_from: suppliers – suppliers include country and lead_time_days, vendors include category

warehouses.summary: Stores information about each warehouse, including its name, region, and storage capacity, enabling analysis of warehouse distribution and capacity utilization.
warehouses.distinct_from: inventory – inventory tracks product stock per warehouse, while warehouses lists warehouse details and capacity.
